title: Edator Edit Pack
description: Deterministic edit-decision list. Claude produces this; renderer/render.js executes it with FFmpeg and makes NO further decisions. Anything the renderer needs must be explicit here. This schema documents what the renderer ACTUALLY does — every field below is executed, none are reserved.

version.description: Edit-pack version. "1.1" is current; "1.0" is an accepted legacy alias (same behaviour).

meta.description: Free-form provenance. Ignored by the renderer; useful for humans and for explaining editorial intent.

sources.description: Named input media. Keys are referenced by timeline segments, pip and the audio bed. Two-roll recordings (e.g. 'cam' + 'screen') share one clock, so the same start/end addresses the same moment in either roll — that's what makes roll-switching and PiP lip-sync for free.

file.description: Path relative to the edit pack, or absolute.

image.description: If true, this source is a still image (B-roll card/diagram). A segment using it is held on-screen for [start,end); the image has no audio, so the segment MUST get sound from the global `audio` bed or its own `audio` override.

audio.description: Optional global audio bed: a source key whose audio plays continuously while the video roll switches above it. Each segment reads this source's audio at the segment's own [start,end]. Use it when rolls share a clock (e.g. bed on 'screen' = the mic) so narration is unbroken across cuts to image B-roll or the other roll. A segment's own `audio` overrides this.

filename.description: Lands in out/ unless absolute. Overridable with --out.

width.description: Canvas width. REQUIRED once the timeline switches rolls or uses pip (frames must share a size).

height.description: Canvas height. REQUIRED once the timeline switches rolls or uses pip.

fps.description: Output frame rate. Also the framerate held image B-roll renders at.

audioFilter.description: FFmpeg audio filter string applied to the concatenated spoken mix, before any music. The LOCKED house default is WARM: "volume=7dB,alimiter=limit=0.95" (raw Rode + gentle lift, NO denoise/EQ/loudnorm — those made it robotic). Don't reach for loudnorm.

declick.description: Whether to apply an automatic ~5ms audio fade at every segment boundary so hard cuts don't click/pop on non-onset joins. On by default — this is hygiene, not a creative choice. Set false only to debug a raw splice.

rawVideoFilter.description: ESCAPE VALVE — opaque ffmpeg filter applied to the WHOLE concatenated picture, after all segments are joined. Unvalidated by design (you're on your own; a bad string fails at ffmpeg). Frames in → frames out, no label refs. Still deterministic because it lives in the pack. Prefer a typed field; if you keep reaching for the same thing here, that's a feature request.

rawAudioFilter.description: ESCAPE VALVE — opaque ffmpeg filter applied to the concatenated spoken mix, AFTER audioFilter (WARM) and BEFORE any music. Unvalidated by design. Audio in → audio out, no label refs. Deterministic because it's in the pack. Don't use it to reinvent loudnorm — the WARM default is deliberate.

music.description: Optional music. Two modes: bookend (set introLen/outroLen) plays over the open + close only, faded, at moderate volume; bed (omit both) is a quiet continuous track under the whole thing. NOTE: house preference is currently NO music — only add when explicitly wanted.

file.description: Path to the music file, relative to the pack or absolute.

introLen.description: Seconds of music over the open (presence enables bookend mode).

outroLen.description: Seconds of music over the close (presence enables bookend mode).

timeline.description: Ordered segments. The output is these clips concatenated in array order, each trimmed to [start,end) from its source. To CUT something, leave a gap between one segment's end and the next's start. That gap-based model is the whole core; everything else (zoom/pip/captions) decorates a segment.

source.description: Key into `sources` — the visible roll for this segment.

start.description: In-point in the source, seconds. (Ignored for image sources except to set duration with `end`.)

end.description: Out-point, seconds. Must be > start. For an image source, end-start sets how long the still is held.

reason.description: Why this segment is kept / what was cut before it. For humans; ignored by renderer.

transition.description: Boundary treatment at the join BEFORE this segment. "dip" (or {"type":"dip","dur":0.4}) dips to black + dips the audio at the join — a soft, deliberate beat between this segment and the previous one, without changing segment timing. xfade (true overlap-crossfade) is not implemented yet. Hard cuts already declick automatically; reach for a dip only when you want the cut to FEEL like a transition.

dur.description: Dip half-length in seconds (each side of the join), default 0.12.

audio.description: Per-segment audio override (a source key). Wins over the global `audio` bed — e.g. an ident's own track, or to let one segment carry its own sound.

rawFilter.description: ESCAPE VALVE — opaque ffmpeg filter spliced in as the LAST transform on this segment's finished frame (after scale/zoom/pip/captions/transition). Unvalidated by design; frames in → frames out, no label refs. Deterministic because it's in the pack. Reach for a typed field (zoom/pip/transition) first — raw is for the thing the vocabulary can't say yet.

zoom.description: Punch-in emphasis. Either a number (uniform scale, e.g. 1.14) or an object. Focus defaults favour the face (y high).

pip.description: Picture-in-picture: overlay another roll in a corner (e.g. the cam in the corner while a diagram/screen fills the frame). Editor captions auto-place in the opposite corner.

width.description: Overlay width as a fraction of canvas width, default 0.25.

margin.description: Inset from the edge as a fraction of canvas width, default 0.02.

captions.description: Burned-in captions for this segment, timed in source-time (same clock as start/end).

style.description: editor = EdAtor's signed chat bubble (Anthropic cream/coral, auto-dodges the PiP) — clearly the AI's voice, NOT the speaker's. label = stumasondev production eyebrow (orange UPPERCASE mono on navy, leading dash). plain = neutral caption.

by.description: editor style only: sender tag, default "EdAtor".

size.description: Font size as a fraction of canvas height (label/plain).
